title: Anthericum simplex
intro: Anthericum simplex, also known as snake's tongue, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, providing relief from pain and discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Anthericum simplex include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Anthericum simplex include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, often used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Anthericum simplex belongs to the family Asphodelaceae, commonly known as the asphodel or lily of the sea family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.
